opening_hook.title: The Missing LSO
opening_hook.scenario: A laser installation proceeds without a designated LSO. During setup, an inexperienced technician accidentally misaligns a Class 4 laser, sending a beam into an occupied area. Who is responsible? What should have been in place?
opening_hook.question: What critical role was missing in this scenario?
opening_hook.feedback: Correct. The LSO is responsible for evaluating laser risks and implementing safety precautions. Their absence left a critical gap in safety oversight.

core_concept.title: What is a Laser Safety Officer?
core_concept.definition: The LSO is an individual designated by the employer who has the authority and responsibility to monitor and enforce the control of laser hazards and to effect the knowledgeable evaluation and control of laser hazards.
key_principles: LSO has EXECUTIVE authority, not just advisory
LSO must be designated by employer/facility management
LSO is responsible for overall laser safety program
ANSI Z136.1 requires LSO for Class 3B and Class 4 operations

lso_vs_lsa.title: LSO vs LSA: Know the Difference

comparison.lso: Executive - can stop work, enforce controls

content.title: Interactive LSO Responsibility Matrix
content.description: Explore the key responsibilities of a Laser Safety Officer organized by category

categories.name: Hazard Evaluation
responsibilities: Evaluate MPE for all exposure conditions
Assess potential for eye and skin exposure

categories.name: Control Measures
responsibilities: Ensure interlocks and safety systems function
Review and approve modifications to systems

categories.name: Training & Authorization
responsibilities: Provide laser safety training to personnel
Authorize personnel to work with lasers

categories.name: Program Administration
responsibilities: Develop and maintain laser safety manual

interactive_features.description: Click each category to reveal detailed responsibilities
Shows how often each duty is performed (daily/weekly/monthly/annual)

ansi_requirements.text: The LSO shall have the necessary training and experience to fulfill the duties of the LSO. The LSO should be knowledgeable in the following areas...

requirements: Understanding of laser physics and operation
Familiarity with different laser types (CW, pulsed, Q-switched)
competency_areas.evidence: Technical training certificate or equivalent experience

requirements: Understanding of ocular anatomy and laser interactions
competency_areas.evidence: Safety training or medical physics background

requirements: Knowledge of ANSI Z136 series standards
Familiarity with state and local requirements

content.title: A Day in the Life of an LSO

scenarios.title: Morning: Pre-Show Checks
scenarios.situation: You arrive at a venue where a laser show will run tonight. What are your first actions?

decision_points.question: First priority upon arrival?
options: Review installation and conduct safety checks
Wait for the operator to arrive
decision_points.feedback: Correct. The LSO must review the installation and verify all safety systems before operation.

decision_points.question: What checks must be completed before the show?
options: E-stop test, alignment check, and MPE verification
Ask the operator if it's safe
decision_points.feedback: Correct. Pre-show checks include E-stop functionality, beam alignment verification, and MPE measurement confirmation.

scenarios.title: Emergency: Suspected Eye Exposure
scenarios.situation: During the show, an audience member reports seeing a bright flash and feels discomfort in their eye.

decision_points.question: Immediate action?
options: Stop the laser show and assess the situation
Continue the show and check later
decision_points.feedback: Correct. Any suspected eye exposure requires immediately stopping the show and investigating.

decision_points.question: What information should be collected?
options: Laser details, exposure circumstances, timing, symptoms
decision_points.feedback: Correct. Complete documentation including laser parameters, exposure conditions, and symptom timeline is essential for medical evaluation.
